title: Brett Anderson
content:
Managing Partner, Alberta Region | Co-Head of Tax
People.ContactEmail banderson@mccarthy.ca
- University of Calgary
A recognized leader in corporate tax planning and dispute resolution, Brett applies a pragmatic, business-minded approach in advising on some of the country’s most complex transactions and disputes.
Brett Anderson, K.C., is McCarthy Tétrault’s Regional Managing Partner for Alberta and the Co-Head of the Firm's National Tax Group, based in the Calgary office. Widely considered one of the most prominent tax planning experts in Canada, Brett provides strategic, value-added advice to clients on complex domestic and cross-border public reorganizations, mergers, acquisitions and international tax matters.
Brett has significant market impact through his counsel to both public and private corporations across industries and throughout Canada. He approaches his practice through the lens of clients’ business and legal needs. Clients trust and rely on Brett’s thorough understanding of the Energy and Infrastructure sectors, giving him unique insights that deliver the most customized, industry-focused advice. He also supports large and mid-market private equity firms on all of their domestic and cross-border transactions.
Brett understands that tax disputes can be overwhelming for clients and that a swift, effective resolution is a business imperative. He leverages his deep knowledge of tax law to provide creative, practical solutions to clients across the entire gamut of tax controversies, including audits, requests for information, objections, and appeals.
Brett’s recent work includes:
- advised Cenovus Energy Inc. on its C$8.6‑billion cash‑and‑stock acquisition of MEG Energy Corp.
- advised Korean Air on its acquisition of a 10% stake in WestJet as part of a US$550‑million transaction with Delta Air Lines for a combined 25% interest
- advised Enbridge Inc. in connection with a landmark transaction in which a partnership of 38 First Nations in British Columbia acquired a 12.5% interest in Enbridge’s Westcoast natural gas pipeline system for subscription receipts of C$736 million
- advised Vale on the approximately US$2.5‑billion sale of a minority stake in Vale Base Metals Limited to Manara Minerals, including a related offtake agreement
- advised Foran Mining Corp. in its acquisition by Eldorado Gold Corp. for C$3.8 billion by way of plan of arrangement
- advised Waste Connections Inc. on its C$1.075‑billion acquisition of 30 energy waste treatment and disposal facilities from Secure Energy Services Inc. through its subsidiary R360 Environmental Solutions Canada Inc.
Ranked as a leading lawyer by Canada’s foremost legal directories, Brett is trusted by clients to help shape their business and tax strategies. He is ranked by Chambers Global and Chambers Canada for Tax, is listed as most frequently recommended for Corporate Tax in The Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory, and has been named a leading lawyer in multiple Lexpert Special Editions , including Finance and Insolvency & Restructuring, as well as Lexpert’s Leading 500 Cross-Border Lawyers guide. Brett is also highly regarded by ITR World Tax and recognized by Best Lawyers in Canada for both Tax Law and Mergers and Acquisitions Law (2025–2026).
Brett is actively involved in the tax planning and disputes landscape in Canada through numerous affiliations, including as a past chair of the Taxation Non-Specialists Sub-Section, Alberta Branch, of the Canadian Bar Association; as a member of the Canadian Petroleum Tax Society; as a member of the Canadian Branch of the International Fiscal Association; and as a member of the Canadian Tax Foundation.

#### Publications and Presentations
- Tax Executives Institute, Co-Presenter, “How to Document and Manage your Files: Dealing with your Professional Liability and Securing the Intended Tax Consequences,” June 12, 2025.
- Canadian Tax Foundation, Annual Conference, Co-Presenter, “The Future of Tax,” November 26, 2023.
- Canadian Petroleum Tax Society, Annual Conference, Co-Presenter, “Interest Deductibility - Proposed Limitations in Budget 2021,” June 3, 2021.
- International Fiscal Association, Annual Conference, Co-Presenter, “Budget 2021 & Other Canadian Law Developments,” May 4-5, 2021.
- Co-Authored, Overview of Canadian Fiscal and Tax Administrative Measures in Response to the COVID-19 Pandemic, Bulletin for International Taxation , Vol. 74, No. 8, July 2020.
- Canadian Tax Foundation, Annual Conference, “Mobility and Executive Compensation,” 2018.
- Canadian Tax Foundation, Annual Conference, Practitioner’s Forum, 2016.
- Canadian Tax Foundation, Annual Conference, “Practical Implementation Issues Arising from the Foreign Affiliate Dumping Rules,” 2014.
- International Fiscal Association, 2014 Joint Canada – USA International Tax Seminar, “Recent U.S. and Canadian Cases Dealing with International Tax Matters,” May 2014.
- Canadian Bar Association, Tax Specialists, “Hot Topics in International Tax,” January 2014.
Brett received his law degree from the University of Calgary. He clerked at the Supreme Court of Canada for Justice John Major in 2003–2004, and was called to the Alberta Bar in 2004. He was appointed King’s Counsel (K.C.) in 2026 to recognize his contributions to the legal profession and public life in Alberta.
